$comment: 2026-05-05 alpha reorder: first tasks = consensus + operator one-pager, then UX/spec waves, then risky duplicate foundation/rail emits at TAIL (tokens+ai rail already wired in repo). Reset lane cursor after pull: npm run lifeos:builder:queue -- --reset-cursor. SSOT queue spec: LIFEOS_DASHBOARD_BUILDER_QUEUE.md. Dual-model consensus: Railway gate-change/run-preset.

tasks.task: Write the LifeOS ALPHA consensus pack after reading injected SSOT + overlays. Sections required: (1) NEEDS ASSESSMENT — what Adam needs to use the prototype today (shell, dashboard, auth, chat/Lumin paths). (2) Alpha bar = MUST (launchable URLs, single happy path flow) vs NICE (density, stubs, telemetry). (3) TWO-AI consensus rule: Conductor supervisor + Railway multi-model council; load-bearing disagreement → POST /api/v1/lifeos/gate-change/run-preset (preset maturity OR program-start) with saved receipt — never IDE-only "council agrees." (4) KNOWN shipped facts: lifeos-dashboard-tokens.css AND lifeos-dashboard-ai-rail.css/js exist and lifeos-dashboard.html already links them and has #lifeos-ai-rail-root — say explicitly so queue does not waste tokens re-emitting the same artifacts first. (5) List the NEXT FIVE queue task IDs that should execute after this file lands (mirror the backlog order already in LIFEOS_DASHBOARD_BUILDER_QUEUE.json after supervisor reorder). Keep under 350 lines.
tasks.spec: Markdown. Headings ###. End with ---METADATA--- JSON: { consensus_models_min: 2, alpha_date_target: string, queue_next_five: string[] }.
tasks.commit_message: [system-build] Alpha consensus pack + dual-AI needs assessment
tasks.notes: Queue head — doc before autonomous runs; anchors council + supervise.

tasks.task: Single-page OPERATOR cheatsheet Adam can paste in notes: bullets only. Include: URLs pattern /overlay/lifeos-app.html and dashboard default query; command npm run lifeos:operational-grade and npm run tsos:builder; where queue JSON lives; how to npm run lifeos:builder:queue -- --reset-cursor after queue edits; Railway council npm run lifeos:gate-change-run -- --preset maturity. Mention login overlay path if relevant. Assume PUBLIC_BASE_URL is operator-specific — use literal placeholder {{PUBLIC_BASE_URL}}.
tasks.spec: Markdown, max ~120 lines, scannable. ---METADATA--- JSON optional.
tasks.notes: Tangible prototype use path for Adam today.

tasks.spec: Markdown. Short sections. Reference the mockup PNG for density study. ---METADATA--- JSON at end.

tasks.task: Produce a gap audit only. The files[] payloads injected below are authoritative — they prove LIFEOS_DASHBOARD_BUILDER_BRIEF.md and LIFEOS_DASHBOARD_BUILDER_QUEUE.md exist on the server. NEVER claim ENOENT/missing SSOT docs. Compare lifeos-dashboard.html and lifeos-app.html against the brief (sidebar, bottom tabs, AI rail direction, light/dark intent, mobile vs desktop). List concrete gaps. Do not rewrite production HTML.
tasks.spec: Markdown only. Sections: Summary, Gaps vs brief, Recommended next queued builds, Open questions if any. If injected file contents contradict prior assumptions, trust injected bodies first. Reference mockup filenames from the brief. End with ---METADATA--- JSON block.
tasks.commit_message: [system-build] Dashboard shell gap audit (supervised 24/7 builder queue)
tasks.notes: Safe first task — documentation only.

tasks.task: Create a new shared CSS token file for LifeOS dashboard shell: semantic color/spacing/radius variables for both light and dark. Use data-theme or .theme-light / .theme-dark on html or body matching patterns already used in lifeos-dashboard.html if present; if unclear, use [data-theme="light"] and [data-theme="dark"] on :root-compatible selectors. Do not break existing overlay pages — this file should be additive.
tasks.spec: Pure CSS file. Include comment header pointing to LIFEOS_DASHBOARD_BUILDER_BRIEF.md. Minimum: --dash-bg, --dash-surface, --dash-text, --dash-muted, --dash-accent, --dash-border, --dash-radius-lg, --dash-space-unit for both themes. End file with newline. No external imports. ---METADATA--- JSON optional for .css — if METADATA required by builder, add HTML comment block at end.
tasks.notes: New small file — easier to validate than rewriting 30k dashboard HTML.

tasks.task: Wire public/shared/lifeos-dashboard-tokens.css into lifeos-dashboard.html with a single link tag in head (correct relative path from /overlay/). Preserve all existing behavior. Apply theme class or data-attribute to html/body if needed for tokens — minimal diff. Ensure both light and dark paths remain reachable per existing dashboard JS/theme logic.
tasks.spec: Minimal HTML edits only. Preserve structure. Full valid HTML document. Do not remove existing scripts. End with ---METADATA--- JSON per builder contract.

tasks.spec: Markdown with Contract, Non-goals, Phases, and Open questions. ---METADATA--- JSON at end.
tasks.notes: Spec before code — reduces unattended HTML thrash.

tasks.task: Create CSS ONLY for the dashboard AI rail: collapsed one-line strip, expanded transcript panel, dock-top and dock-bottom variants, safe-area insets for notched/mobile, respects token variables from lifeos-dashboard-tokens.css and [data-theme] if present on html/body. No JavaScript.
tasks.spec: Pure CSS file. Leading comment cites DASHBOARD_AI_RAIL_CONTRACT.md. Class names prefixed lifeos-ai-rail-*. No external @import except optional reference only (prefer none). End with newline.

Respect reduced-motion.
